class.name: AudioStreamEffect
class.brief_description: Applies effects to an audio stream.
class.description: [AudioStreamEffect] applies [AudioEffect]s to a child [AudioStream]. Effects are applied in the order in which they are listed. [b]Note:[/b] Different effects can require vastly different amounts of processing power. Be mindful of how often intensive effects such as reverb and delay are used with your set-up.

method.name: add_effect
method.description: Inserts an effect at the specified index. If [param index] is less than 0, the effect will be added to the end.

method.name: get_effect
method.description: Returns the audio effect at the specified index.

method.name: get_effect_bypass_enabled
method.description: Returns if the audio effect at the specified index is currently bypassed.

method.name: move_effect
method.description: Moves the effect at [param index_from] to [param index_to]. If [param index_to] is below 0, the effect will be moved to the end of the list. [b]Note:[/b] Due to the way elements are moved, if [param index_to] is greater than [param index_from], the final index of the moved item will be one less than [param index_to].

method.name: remove_effect
method.description: Removes the effect at the specified index. Shifts all elements past the specified index back.

method.name: set_effect
method.description: Sets the audio effect at the specified index.

method.name: set_effect_bypass_enabled
method.description: Enables or disables bypass on the effect at the specified index. Bypassing an effect disables the effect's influence on the sound, essentially turning it off.

members.member: The total amount of effect slots in the [AudioStreamEffect].

If [code]true[/code], effects that are bypassed will still be processed. Useful for effects like reverb, where pausing processing will "freeze" the state of the effect, and un-bypassing will resume the effect from where it left off, potentially causing a sudden burst of sound. Setting this to [code]true[/code] will negate any performance improvements gained from enabling bypass on effects, as effects will still be processed.
The [AudioStream] resource to play and apply effects to.
The volume curve to apply to the tail as it fades out. Set to a low value such as 0.0001 to mimic a hard cut when the tail ends.
The amount of time, in seconds, to wait after the child stream stops before stopping this stream. Useful for letting effects like reverb ring out.
